text: The surgical field reveals a significant amount of pericholecystic fluid and edema surrounding the gallbladder, indicating inflammation and limited working space.

The surgeon identifies the duct positioned deep within the surgical field and attempts to maneuver the artery to clear the area for further dissection.
The surgeon uses a round tip scissor to continue dissection, maneuvering around the anatomical structures in the surgical field.

The surgeon visually identifies pericholecystic fluid surrounding the gallbladder, using ICG guidance for enhanced visualization.

The surgeon uses gentle cautery pumps to free the cystic plate, which is visibly edematous, from surrounding tissue.
The surgical field shows significant edema and vascularity in the cystic plate area, with visible swelling and blood vessels.
